supporting_material_rows.detail: the bridge contract must stay green before the direct and resumable carriers can be published as separate contract-bearing lanes
the semantic-preservation audit must stay green so the two carriers do not collapse across incompatible state and continuation semantics
the control-plane proof must stay green so the split remains tied to one machine-owned decision contract rather than host-owned route collapse
the article acceptance gate must stay green so the direct carrier remains the canonical article-equivalent route only
the declared `TCM.v1` runtime contract must stay green so the resumable carrier remains explicit instead of being inferred loosely from the direct route
the historical closeout must stay green so the resumable carrier continues to own the bounded theory/operator universality lane instead of the direct article-equivalent lane
the March 20 rebase audit remains observational context only here and motivates the explicit carrier split without substituting for the proof-carrying contract rows
the transformer-stack boundary remains observational context only here and records the canonical route boundary that the direct carrier is not allowed to widen around

primary_carrier_rows.detail: the direct carrier owns only the bounded article-equivalent, direct-route-minimality, single-run-no-spill, and declared-machine-matrix truths. It does not own resumable universality or Turing-completeness closeout truth.
the resumable carrier owns only the theory/operator universality and bounded Turing-completeness closeout truths. It does not own direct article-equivalent same-run truth.

claim_class_binding_rows.detail: claim class `bounded_article_equivalence` is bound only to the direct article-equivalent carrier and may not be transferred onto the resumable carrier by implication.
claim class `direct_route_minimality` is bound only to the direct article-equivalent carrier and may not be transferred onto the resumable carrier by implication.
claim class `single_run_no_spill_article_execution` is bound only to the direct article-equivalent carrier and may not be transferred onto the resumable carrier by implication.
claim class `declared_cpu_machine_matrix_reproducibility` is bound only to the direct article-equivalent carrier and may not be transferred onto the resumable carrier by implication.
claim class `theory_green_universality` is bound only to the resumable universality carrier and may not be transferred onto the direct article-equivalent carrier by implication.
claim class `operator_green_universality` is bound only to the resumable universality carrier and may not be transferred onto the direct article-equivalent carrier by implication.
claim class `bounded_turing_completeness_closeout` is bound only to the resumable universality carrier and may not be transferred onto the direct article-equivalent carrier by implication.

validation_rows.detail: helper substitution remains quarantined, so the carrier split cannot be redefined through hidden host helpers.
route drift remains rejected, so the direct carrier stays tied to one fixed canonical route id.
continuation abuse remains quarantined, so direct and resumable carriers cannot be collapsed through hidden continuation semantics.
semantic drift remains blocked, so the two carriers retain their declared semantics instead of collapsing into one blended claim class.
carrier collapse is refused: direct and resumable claim sets remain disjoint, every claim class binds to exactly one carrier, and transfer by implication is blocked.
proof-carrying artifacts remain distinct from observational context while the split is published as its own contract.
the carrier split remains bounded: capability plane stays reserved, and rebased universality, served/public universality, weighted plugin control, and arbitrary software capability all stay blocked.

claim_boundary: this contract publishes only the explicit split between direct article-equivalent carrier truths and bounded resumable universality carrier truths on the post-`TAS-186` bridge machine identity. It binds each claim class to one carrier and refuses later collapse into one undifferentiated route claim. It does not by itself rebind the universal-machine proof, reissue the universality witness suite, publish the rebased verdict split, admit served/public universality, admit weighted plugin control, or admit arbitrary software capability.
summary: Post-article carrier split contract keeps supporting_materials=8/8, primary_carriers=2/2, claim_bindings=7/7, carrier_split_status=Green, carrier_collapse_refused=true, and carrier_split_publication_complete=true.
